text.title: stakeholder-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for stakeholder-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM hallucinating stakeholders that "should" exist for a project type ("Compliance Officer", "Data Privacy Lead") without a source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the model to cite an actual source — meeting notes, org chart, CODEOWNERS — before adding a row. Hallucinated stakeholders lead to wasted engagement cycles on non-existent relationships.

failure-mode.description: Models defaulting to Supporter for attitude when asked "is this exec a supporter?" because of sycophantic training.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the model to mark Unknown unless evidence is cited; the stakeholder-lint.py script enforces this at commit time. Unknown attitudes must be resolved through 1:1 conversation, not LLM inference.

failure-mode.description: Power/interest classifications inferred from title alone in a matrix org where a "Senior Manager" has more real influence than a "VP".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feed actual org-chart text or interview snippets rather than job titles. Title-based classification is wrong in consensus-driven and matrix orgs.

failure-mode.description: Cron-driven weekly stakeholder sentiment scan of Slack messages auto-escalating on mis-classified Resistor signals.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Queue sentiment findings for human review, never auto-escalate. A false Resistor classification triggering an escalation can damage a relationship the system was meant to protect.

failure-mode.description: Long-context dumps of the full register into every agent prompt, wasting tokens and leaking PII to downstream agents.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pass only the relevant slice (one quadrant, or one engagement tier) to each agent. The register as a whole is a program-management artifact, not an agent context blob.

failure-mode.description: Register rot: the doc is built once at project kick-off and never updated.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: By month 3, the named exec sponsor has left the company and the register still drives the weekly update email. Tie register review to the same cadence as roadmap review.

failure-mode.description: Power/interest grids hide attitude: a high-power stakeholder coded as "Manage Closely" but secretly a Resistor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always carry an explicit attitude column (Supporter / Neutral / Resistor) and color it. Attitude is the third dimension the grid cannot show.

failure-mode.description: Communication-plan theatre: an elaborate matrix that nobody actually executes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the PM isn't running the weekly sync the matrix promises, the matrix is a lie. Reduce cadence to what will actually happen.

failure-mode.description: Over-engagement of Low Power, Low Interest stakeholders out of politeness.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Burns PM cycles and dilutes signal. The whole point of the grid is to deprioritize. Manage this quadrant with periodic check-ins, not active engagement.

failure-mode.description: One-way "stakeholder updates" treated as engagement: a newsletter is information, not engagement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Engagement requires bidirectional input loops (decisions, feedback, escalation paths). Frame updates as such, not as engagement substitutes.

failure-mode.description: Cross-cultural blind spots: Power is more diffuse in matrix orgs and consensus-driven cultures.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A simple grid encodes a Western-corporate hierarchy assumption. Annotate, don't auto-trust. Feed actual org-chart data instead of inferring from titles.
